All assert targets in tests (file path, function names like `getPopoverMiddlewares`, `usePopover`, string literals like `limitShi

no_solution_leakage.reason: The instruction explicitly states the fix: 'when the shift middleware executes before flip, it pushes the popover into the viewport first. This prevents flip from detecting an overflow condition.' Combined with the reference to floating-ui docs on 'combining flip with shift' and the clear description that the ordering is wrong, this directly tells the agent to move flip before shift. The Bug Description section essentially says 'swap the order of shift and flip middleware blocks.'

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: The tests check that the flip block appears before the shift block by line number, and verify both blocks contain the expected implementation patterns. Any correct fix that reorders flip before shift while preserving the block implementations would pass. The tests don't assert exact line numbers, only relative ordering.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: The primary fail-to-pass tests (`test_flip_middleware_before_shift`, `test_flip_middleware_has_correct_body`, `test_shift_middleware_has_correct_body`) are purely text-based: they read the file content and check for string patterns like `'if (middlewaresOptions.flip)' in line` and `'flip()' in content`. While there are pass-to-pass tests that run eslint, tsc, prettier, and jest as subprocesses, the core fail-to-pass tests only do text matching with no code execution.

test_not_tautological.reason: The f2p tests check that flip comes before shift in the source code. In the buggy version (shift before flip), `test_flip_middleware_before_shift` would fail because `flip_line > shift_line`. A stub or no-op implementation wouldn't pass because the tests require specific middleware block patterns to exist in the file.

anti_cheating_measures.reason: The solution file exists at `/workspace/mantine` (the repo is cloned there) and `solution/solve.sh` is in the container. An agent could read `/solution/solve.sh` to see the exact patch. Additionally, the tests are purely text-based checks on source code, so an agent could just read the test file to determine exactly what string patterns to produce.

behavior_in_tests.reason: All requirements from instruction.md are tested: middleware ordering (flip before shift), preservation of both shift (limitShift, padding:5) and flip implementations, ESLint, TypeScript type checking, Prettier formatting, and existing Popover tests passing.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are well-named (`test_flip_middleware_before_shift`, `test_shift_middleware_has_correct_body`, etc.), each has a detailed docstring explaining what it checks and why, and they are logically grouped by concern (ordering, implementation, syntax, linting, formatting, unit tests).
